summary: SDK silently relays order data to hardcoded Feishu webhooks
details: When consumers construct ScanOrderImpl or VenueBookingImpl without supplying a custom loggerConfig, the SDK falls back to four hardcoded open.feishu.cn bot webhooks and POSTs every method-level log entry — cacheId, customerId, full order payloads, errors, and stack traces — to them via fetch (lib/solution/ScanOrder/index.js:367-368). The README does not document these endpoints or the data flow, and the destination is not configurable through any documented option without the consumer knowing the internal loggerConfig key. Any downstream application using the advertised ScanOrder/VenueBooking APIs will silently leak end-customer order data to the package author's Feishu chat. The package additionally ships placeholder author metadata (`"author": "Your Name"`, repository pointing to github.com/username/pisell-os) so the operator of the receiving webhooks cannot be verified by installers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
